title: Use an Oven Safely
summary: Preheat and use an oven for baking or roasting without injury.
learnerPromise: You will be able to preheat an oven, place food inside safely, and remove it without burns or accidents.
whyItMatters: Ovens reach high temperatures that can cause serious burns. Learning safe setup, placement, and removal habits helps you bake and roast food with much lower risk.
realLifeUses: Baking a frozen pizza at the temperature on the package
Roasting vegetables for a side dish
Baking a simple cake or cookies from a mix
Reheating food in an oven-safe dish
result: Food placed in and removed from a preheated oven safely, with no burns or spills.
youWillLearn: Preheat the oven to the correct temperature
Use oven mitts to handle hot dishes and racks
Place and remove dishes without touching hot surfaces
Turn the oven off after use and leave the door closed while it cools
miniChallenge: Preheat your oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit (175 Celsius), place an oven-safe empty dish inside for one minute, then remove it safely using oven mitts. Turn the oven off afterward.
steps: Make sure the oven is empty. Remove any items stored inside.
Set the oven to the temperature you need and let it preheat fully.
Open the door slowly. Stand to the side to avoid the blast of hot air.
Place the dish on the center rack using oven mitts. Close the door gently.
When done, turn the oven off. Open the door carefully, remove the dish with mitts, and let the oven cool with the door closed or slightly open as needed.
completionCriteria: You preheated the oven to the correct temperature
You placed and removed the dish using oven mitts without touching the rack or oven walls
You turned the oven off after use
commonProblems: Reaching into the oven without mitts and burning hands or arms
Placing a cold glass dish into a hot oven, which can cause it to shatter
Opening the oven door quickly and getting a face full of hot steam
tips: Use two oven mitts—one for each hand—when removing heavy or hot items.
Let frozen or refrigerated dishes warm slightly before putting them in a hot oven.
Keep children and pets away from the oven while it is on and while it cools down.
helpBoundary: Ask for help if you get a burn larger than a small coin, if the oven smokes or smells like gas, if you see sparks, or if a dish breaks inside the oven. For gas smells you cannot stop, leave the area and call for help.
